title: Fixing Type Inference for Function Parameters

statement.markdown: You are given a function that takes an object as an argument. The function is supposed to return a new object with a single property, `name`, which is the `name` property of the input object. However, the function is not correctly inferring the type of the input object. Fix the function to correctly infer the type of the input object and return a new object with the correct type.
statement.outputFormat: A new object with a `name` property of the same type as the input object
statement.notes: The function should not throw any errors

examples.explanation: The function should return a new object with the same type as the input object
The function should return a new object with the same type as the input object

hints.text: Check the type of the input object
Use the correct syntax to infer the type of the input object

editorial.title: Editorial for Fixing Type Inference for Function Parameters
editorial.intuition: The function should correctly infer the type of the input object and return a new object with the correct type
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to use the `any` type, but this will not provide type safety
editorial.betterSolution: Use the `as` keyword to infer the type of the input object
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `as` keyword and the correct syntax to infer the type of the input object
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function will correctly infer the type of the input object and return a new object with the correct type
